findings.summary: Case reports document digoxin toxicity emerging in patients consuming licorice-containing products, attributed to licorice-induced hypokalemia potentiating digoxin's inhibitory effect on cardiac Na+/K+-ATPase.

Pharmacokinetic studies indicate glycyrrhetinic acid, the active licorice metabolite, can modulate P-glycoprotein activity, potentially altering absorption and tissue distribution of P-gp substrate drugs such as digoxin.
findings.citation: Wang et al., Eur J Pharm Sci 2017

findings.summary: Chronic licorice ingestion at moderate-to-high doses has repeatedly been linked to pseudohyperaldosteronism, with hypokalemia, hypertension, and edema reported across multiple case series.
